sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The loop stopped because no g frame appeared when selecting the green at (16, 38). This is different from Level 1. Let me check the current state:
- 3 greens remain: (16, 38), (16, 44), (52, 44)
- No g frame appeared when selecting (16, 38)
Maybe the mechanic changed or I need to select a different green object. Let me try selecting the green at (52, 44) which is in a different grid area. Or maybe I need to select (16, 44) instead.
Looking at the image, I see:
- Top grid: 2 green objects at (16, 38) and (16, 44)
- Bottom-right grid: 1 green object at (52, 44)
- Orange/yellow square in the middle
Maybe the issue is that (16, 38) and (16, 44) are in the same row and adjacent, so selecting one doesn't produce a g frame because they need to be moved from different grids first. Let me try selecting the one in the bottom grid at (52, 44).

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: The loop stopped - no g frame appeared when selecting green at (16, 38). 3 greens remain: (16, 38), (16, 44), (52, 44). Maybe need to select a green from a different grid section.
Plan: Try selecting the green at (52, 44) in the bottom-right grid to see if a g frame appears.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
def get_centers(seg, color):
    nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == color]
    centers = []
    for n in nodes:
        center = (sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary'])//len(n['boundary']), sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary'])//len(n['boundary']))
        centers.append(center)
    return centers

# Try selecting green at (52, 44) - the one in the bottom grid
print("Selecting green at (52, 44)...")
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 52, 'col': 44}])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
g_centers = get_centers(seg, 'g')
print(f"g frame centers: {g_centers}")

if g_centers:
    target_r = sum(c[0] for c in g_centers) // len(g_centers)
    target_c = sum(c[1] for c in g_centers) // len(g_centers)
    print(f"Target: ({target_r}, {target_c})")
    
    # Click target
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': target_r, 'col': target_c}])
    
    seg2 = current_frame.segmentation
    green_centers = get_centers(seg2, 'N')
    print(f"Greens after: {green_centers}")
    print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")
    
    # Continue
    if len(green_centers) > 1 and not last_action_result.get('level_completed', False):
        print(f"\nSelecting green at {green_centers[0]}...")
        action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': green_centers[0][0], 'col': green_centers[0][1]}])
        
        seg3 = current_frame.segmentation
        g_centers3 = get_centers(seg3, 'g')
        print(f"g frame: {g_centers3}")
        
        if g_centers3:
            target_r3 = sum(c[0] for c in g_centers3) // len(g_centers3)
            target_c3 = sum(c[1] for c in g_centers3) // len(g_centers3)
            action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': target_r3, 'col': target_c3}])
            
            seg4 = current_frame.segmentation
            green_centers4 = get_centers(seg4, 'N')
            print(f"Greens after: {green_centers4}")
            print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
